title: ephedra vulgaris subsp. helvetica
intro: Ephedra vulgaris subsp. helvetica, also known as the Alpine Ephedra, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ephedraceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate the respiratory system and increase circulation. The health benefits of Alpine Ephedra include relief from asthma, bronchitis, and other respiratory issues, as well as its potential to boost energy and aid in weight loss. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusion, tincture, or tea, and has also been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its stimulating and decongestant properties.
